meanings.definition: the heavy fire of artillery to saturate an area rather than hit a specific target
examples: they laid down a barrage in front of the advancing troops
the shelling went on for hours without pausing
The enemy has been shelling us all day
blast a passage through the mountain

meanings.definition: fall out of the pod or husk
hit the pitches of hard and regularly
examples: He shelled the pitcher for eight runs in the first inning

meanings.definition: look for and collect shells by the seashore
come out better in a competition, race, or conflict
examples: Agassi beat Becker in the tennis championship
Harvard defeated Yale in the last football game

meanings.definition: remove from its shell or outer covering
